# sphinx-ci — Full Documentation for LLMs

## Overview
sphinx-ci is a CI/CD tool that generates AI-powered code comprehension quizzes from Pull Request diffs on GitHub. It is free, open source, and designed to encourage developers to understand their own code before merging.

## Architecture

### Two-part system
1. **Central hub** (sphinx-ci.dev): A Next.js 14 web application that:
   - Authenticates users via GitHub OAuth
   - Lets admins configure repos and generate API keys
   - Receives PR diffs from GitHub Actions
   - Generates quiz questions using Anthropic Claude
   - Hosts the quiz UI
   - Reports results back to GitHub (PR comments + status checks)

2. **GitHub Action**: A workflow file (.github/workflows/pr-quiz.yml) added to each protected repo that:
   - Triggers on `/sphinx` comments on PRs
   - Extracts the code diff
   - Sends it to the hub
   - Posts the quiz link as a PR comment

### Database schema
- **User**: GitHub OAuth users (id, name, email, image, githubId, githubLogin)
- **Account**: OAuth accounts linked to users (provider, access_token, etc.)
- **Team**: Repo configurations (name=repo full name, apiKey, userId, anthropicApiKey encrypted, quizConfig JSON)
- **Quiz**: Generated quizzes (repo, prNumber, headSha, prTitle, diff, questions JSON, status, score, attempts, maxAttempts, callbackToken, expiresAt)
- **QuizStatus**: PENDING | PASSED | FAILED | EXPIRED

### Quiz configuration (per repo)
- numQuestions: 5-20 (default 10)
- passingScore: 50-90% (default 70%)
- maxAttempts: 1-5 (default 3)
- language: "en" | "fr" (default "en")
- keyword: trigger keyword (default "/sphinx")

### Security
- Correct answers are never sent to the browser before submission
- Score is calculated entirely server-side
- New questions are generated on each retry attempt
- Anthropic API keys are encrypted with AES-256-GCM before storage
- Rate limiting: 10 quizzes per hour per API key (Redis if available, DB fallback)
- Quiz pages require GitHub authentication

### API Endpoints

#### POST /api/quiz
Creates a new quiz. Called by the GitHub Action.
- Header: X-API-Key (team API key)
- Body: { repo, pr_number, head_sha, pr_title, diff, files_changed, callback_token, anthropic_api_key }
- Returns: { quiz_id, quiz_url, expires_at } or { skipped: true }

#### POST /api/quiz/:id/submit
Submits quiz answers. Called from the browser.
- Body: { answers: number[] }
- Returns: { score, passed, correct, total, attempts_remaining, results[] }
- Side effects: Updates GitHub status check, posts PR comment

#### GET /api/quiz/:id/regenerate
Generates new questions for a retry attempt.
- Returns: { success: true }

#### POST /api/keys
Creates a new team/API key for a repo. Requires OAuth session.
- Body: { repo, config }
- Returns: { id, apiKey, repo }

#### PUT /api/keys/:id
Updates repo configuration. Requires OAuth session.
- Body: { config }

#### PATCH /api/keys/:id
Regenerates (resets) the API key. Requires OAuth session.
- Returns: { apiKey }

#### DELETE /api/keys/:id
Revokes a team and deletes associated quizzes. Requires OAuth session.

### GitHub Action Workflow
Trigger: issue_comment (created) containing '/sphinx'
The workflow:
1. Gets PR details (head_sha, base_sha, title)
2. Checks out the repo
3. Sets pending status on the commit
4. Extracts code diff (filtered by file extensions, max 12KB)
5. Sends to hub POST /api/quiz
6. Posts quiz link as PR comment

### Environment Variables
Hub (Vercel):
- DATABASE_URL: PostgreSQL connection string
- NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL: Public URL of the hub
- GITHUB_CLIENT_ID: OAuth App client ID
- GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET: OAuth App client secret
- AUTH_SECRET: Session encryption secret
- ENCRYPTION_KEY (optional): Key for encrypting Anthropic API keys
- UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URL (optional): Redis for rate limiting
- UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_TOKEN (optional): Redis token

Repo (GitHub Actions secrets/variables):
- PR_QUIZ_API_KEY (secret): Team API key from sphinx-ci dashboard
- ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (secret): User's Anthropic API key
- PR_QUIZ_HUB_URL (variable): URL of the hub (e.g., https://sphinx-ci.dev)

### Internationalization
The app supports English (default) and French. Language is stored in a cookie and switchable via a button in the navbar.

### Team/Organization Support
- One admin configures repos, all team members can take quizzes
- Devs see repos configured by others in read-only mode
- Organization repos require OAuth App approval by org admin
- Only the admin who configured a repo can edit/revoke it
